title: Cozy Smoky French Casabe Gratin
description: A smoky and springy French dish featuring casabe, wood-fired-baked to perfection, kosher-friendly and perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 3 minutes until translucent.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 4 minutes until tender.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked mushroom mixture, smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and chopped thyme.
In a separate bowl, mix the shredded gruyere cheese and crushed casabe.
In a baking dish, create a layer of the mushroom mixture, followed by a layer of the cheese and casabe mixture, and repeat until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the gratin in the wood-fired oven for 25 minutes until golden brown and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
